task_completion.description: The agent visualizes the chest CT in a sagittal plane and places measurement landmarks on the thoracic spine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D Slicer (or similar medical imaging software) UI showing a sagittal view of the spine with angle measurement lines or fiducial points placed on the upper and lower thoracic vertebrae.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement landmarks to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command, script, or Slicer save dialog showing 'kyphosis_landmarks.mrk.json' being saved to '~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/'.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the kyphosis report file with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal 'cat' command, or Python script output showing 'kyphosis_report.json' created in '~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/' with keys: superior_vertebra, inferior_vertebra, cobb_angle_degrees, and classification.

task_completion.description: The report correctly identifies an acceptable superior vertebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'superior_vertebra' field in the report is set to one of the acceptable upper thoracic levels (T2, T3, T4, T5, or T6).

task_completion.description: The report correctly identifies an acceptable inferior vertebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'inferior_vertebra' field in the report is set to one of the acceptable lower thoracic/lumbar levels (T9, T10, T11, T12, or L1).

task_completion.description: The report contains the measured Cobb angle in degrees.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'cobb_angle_degrees' field in the report contains a numerical value representing the measured angle.

task_completion.description: The report contains the correct clinical classification based on the angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'classification' field in the report contains a classification string (e.g., 'Normal' if the angle is between 20 and 45 degrees).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the imaging software to measure the angle rather than guessing or hardcoding values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the 3D Slicer angle measurement tool actively used on the CT scan, with visible measurement lines and an angle readout.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent opened and viewed the CT scan before creating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CT scan must be visible in the application window in at least one screenshot prior to the generation or editing of 'kyphosis_report.json'.

integrity.description: Verifies the landmarks file was generated by the software or a script interacting with the software's data, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The '.mrk.json' file should contain complex coordinate data typical of Slicer markups, or the Slicer save dialog/Python console should be used to export it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Acceptable superior vertebrae for thoracic kyphosis measurement are T2, T3, T4, T5, and T6. Acceptable inferior vertebrae are T9, T10, T11, T12, and L1. The normal range for the thoracic kyphosis Cobb angle is 20 to 45 degrees. If the measured angle falls within this range, the correct classification is 'Normal'. An inter-observer measurement tolerance of 8 degrees is acceptable.
